summary: Formatting-only cleanup across sysdolphin baselib sources and headers, primarily reordering include blocks to match the repository formatter's conventions. The PR touched 29 files under src/sysdolphin/baselib with no review discussion and no evidence of functional code changes in the provided diff excerpt.
what_changed: Ran the repository formatter over sysdolphin baselib files.
Moved each file's own header toward the top in many .c files, e.g. cobj.c now includes "cobj.h" before other baselib headers, and gobj.c now starts with "gobj.h".
Separated local project includes from system, Dolphin SDK, MetroTRK, MSL, and placeholder includes with blank lines.
Reordered mixed include groups in headers such as controller.h, displayfunc.h, psstructs.h, shadow.h, and mtx.h.
Preserved existing declarations, globals, typedefs, and function bodies in the visible excerpt; changes shown are include ordering and whitespace only.
smart_moves: Isolated formatting into its own PR, which reduces noise for future functional decompilation reviews.
Applied formatting consistently across a tightly related subsystem rather than making opportunistic style edits inside matching work.
Let `make format` enforce include layout, avoiding subjective manual style churn.
decomp_lessons: Keep formatter-driven include reordering separate from matching or type-recovery PRs so future diffs remain attributable.
When touching baselib sources, expect the formatter to prefer a file's primary local header near the top followed by related local headers and then external SDK/system headers.
Formatting-only PRs can still affect many files, so later archaeology should not assume broad file coverage implies semantic changes.
For future decomp work, run `make format` before submission to avoid unrelated include-order churn during review.
naming_conventions: No new symbols or naming conventions were introduced by this PR.
Existing HSD naming remains visible in unchanged declarations, including HSD_ClassInfo, HSD_JObjInfo, HSD_PObjInfo, HSD_ObjAllocData, HSD_VIInfo, HSD_PadLibData, and HSD_FlushType.
Existing baselib object-module naming remains consistent across files: cobj, fobj, gobj, jobj, mobj, pobj, robj, tobj, tev, texp, shadow, rumble, displayfunc, initialize, and mtx.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactic changes are evidenced.
The diff excerpt does not show changes to function bodies, pragmas beyond existing MUST_MATCH context, data layout, or generated assembly-sensitive expressions.
The main reusable tactic is process-oriented: run formatting as a separate cleanup pass so matching diffs remain focused.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale beyond the title `Run make format` is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify sysdolphin baselib include ordering or formatter rules.
Search for functional baselib PRs near PR 1121 that may have been preceded or followed by this formatting cleanup.
Check whether `make format` conventions are documented for primary header order in C files and project-vs-system include grouping.
